  -r, --recurse                 (Default: false) Recursively select

                                the corresponding transformed file.

                                output rather than compact output (all white

                                true. --pretty-print and --minify cannot be
                                specified together.

  -m, --minify                  (Default: false) Produce compact JSON output
                                (all white space removed) rather than

                                nor --minify is specified, --pretty-print is set
                                to true. --pretty-print and --minify cannot be
                                specified together.

  -f, --force                   (Default: false) Force overwrite of output file
                                if it exists.

  --insert                      Optionally present data, expressed as a
                                semicolon-delimited list, that should be
                                inserted into the log file. Valid values include
                                Hashes, TextFiles, BinaryFiles,
                                EnvironmentVariables, RegionSnippets,
                                ContextRegionSnippets, Guids,
                                VersionControlInformation, and
                                NondeterministicProperties.

  --remove                      Optionally present data, expressed as a
                                semicolon-delimited list, that should be not be
                                persisted to or which should be removed from the
                                log file. Valid values include Hashes,
                                TextFiles, BinaryFiles, EnvironmentVariables,
                                RegionSnippets, ContextRegionSnippets, Guids,
                                VersionControlInformation, and
                                NondeterministicProperties.

  -u, --uriBaseIds              A key + value pair that defines a uriBaseId and
                                its corresponding local file path. E.g.,

                                and Current

  --help                        Display this help screen.

  --version                     Display version information.

  <files> (pos. 0)              Required. Files to process (wildcards ? and *
                                allowed).

staticCommands.name: absoluteuri
staticCommands.description: Turn all relative Uris into absolute URIs (to be used after rebaseUri is run)

staticCommands.name: convert
staticCommands.description: Convert a tool output log to SARIF format.

options.description: The tool format of the input file. Must be one of: AndroidStudio, ClangAnalyzer, CppCheck, ContrastSecurity, FlawFinderCsv, Fortify, FortifyFpr, FxCop, PREfast, Pylint, SemmleQL, StaticDriverVerifier, TSLint, or a tool format for which a plugin assembly provides the converter.
Normalize converted output to conform to GitHub Advanced Security code scanning ingestion requirements.
Path to plugin assembly containing converter types.

staticCommands.name: export-config
staticCommands.description: Export rule options to an XML or JSON file that can be edited and used to configure subsequent analysis.

values.name: outputfilepath
values.description: Output path for exported analysis options

options.description: The file format to persist settings to (if the format cannot be inferred from the file name extension).

staticCommands.name: export-docs
staticCommands.description: Export the documentation for the analysis rules to a Markdown file.

options.description: Path to the generated Markdown file. Default: Rules.md in the current directory.

staticCommands.name: export-rules
staticCommands.description: Export rules metadata to a SARIF or SonarQube XML file.

values.name: outputfilepath
values.description: Output path for exported analysis options. Use a .json or .sarif extension to produce SARIF. Use .xml to produce a SonarQube rule descriptor file.

staticCommands.name: export-validation-config
staticCommands.description: Export validation rule options to an XML or JSON file that can be edited and used to configure subsequent analysis.

staticCommands.name: export-validation-docs
staticCommands.description: Export the documentation for the validation rules to a Markdown file.

staticCommands.name: export-validation-rules
staticCommands.description: Export validation rules metadata to a SARIF or SonarQube XML file.

staticCommands.name: file-work-items
staticCommands.description: Send SARIF results to a work item tracking system such as GitHub or Azure DevOps

options.description: A path to an XML configuration file that will be used to drive work item creation.
File work items for both new and unchanged baseline state results. If an unchanged result already has an associated work item, a new work item will not be created.
The absolute URI of the project in which the work items are to be filed, for example https://dev.azure.com/{org}/{project} or https://github.com/{org}/{repo}.
Do not validate the SARIF log file before filing.

Apply a splitting strategy to each input log file in order to break the file into multiple bugs. Must be one of None, PerRun, PerResult, PerRunPerRule, PerRunPerTargetPerRule or PerRunPerTarget. By default ('None'), no splitting strategy is applied (i.e. each input file will be filed as a single bug).
Enrich work item to be created with existing work item metadata for any result workItemUri.

staticCommands.name: match-results-forward
staticCommands.description: Track results run over run by persisting IDs and then matching them forward

values.name: <currentFiles>
values.description: Path(s) to SARIF log(s) comprising the current set of results, without result matching information

options.description: File path to output the annotated SARIF log with result matching information.  Defaults to <currentFile>-annotated.sarif
Path to a SARIF log containing the previous set of results with result matching annotations on a target (or empty if no previous log exists)

staticCommands.name: merge
staticCommands.description: Merge multiple SARIF files into one.

options.description: Merge log files with no results into the final log.
Merge runs of the same tool + verion combination (requires eliding run-specific details such as invocations data.
File name to write merged content to.
Apply a splitting strategy to the merged file. Must be one of None or PerRule. By default ('None'), no splitting strategy is applied (i.e. all input files will be merged into a single log).

staticCommands.description: Extract a subset of results from a source SARIF file.

values.name: <inputFile>
values.description: A path to a SARIF file to process.

options.description: Number of results from index to include.
0-based index of first result to include.
A file path of the SARIF subset output file.
Force overwrite of output file if it exists.
Target map size relative to file size (0.01 is 1%)
0-based index of the run to copy.

staticCommands.name: query
staticCommands.description: Find the matching subset of a SARIF file and output it or log it.

values.name: <inputFile>
values.description: A path to a SARIF file to process.

options.description: Result Expression to Evaluate (ex: (BaselineState != 'Unchanged'))
Force overwrite of output file if it exists.
Whether to return a non-zero exit code if the count exceeds a threshold
A file path of the SARIF subset output file, if desired.
Produce pretty-printed JSON output rather than compact form.
Exit Code is the count of matching results
Whether to write matching results to the console

staticCommands.description: Rebase the URIs in one or more sarif files.

options.description: Variable to use for the base path token (e.x. 'SRCROOT')
Base path value to use while rebasing all paths.  E.x. 'C:\bld\1234\bin\'
All relative uris will be rebased to the base-path-value if true. Default is false.

values.name: folder-path

options.description: Folder Path where output will be written.
